$schema_note: AIMR (AI Model Router) capability registry v2. Top-level `models` is the submodel catalog: cost data (API $/MTok where metered, quota-draw weight where subscription), effort levels, and when-to-use. Top-level `pools` is the availability layer's metadata: how scripts/aimr_doctor.py detects each account pool's CLI, auth, and usage — probe RECIPES live here with source + confidence; probe RESULTS never do (quality rankings move slowly and belong in this file; availability is probed live at routing time). Capabilities rank `providers` best-first; each provider carries four contracts: invocation (how to call), artifact (what comes back: `type` is the terminal artifact class — file|url|worktree — and `delivery` is what work remains after the provider returns: path|download-required|stdout|worktree-review), cost (which account pool it draws), score (benchmarked, dated, auditable to a named suite — or explicitly unbenchmarked/seeded, never invented). `<skill-dir>` in command templates means the directory containing this registry, wherever the skill is installed. LLM-backed providers reference a `models` entry via `model` (+ `effort`); media providers are their own model. `human_options` lists quality-winning providers agents cannot drive — surfaced to humans, never routed; their scores follow the same auditability rules. Every cost number carries source + confidence; null means unknown, not zero.
$scope_note: Scope (2026-07-13): AIMR evaluates agent-drivable CLIs and their added capabilities — Codex and Claude are the core lanes; Grok CLI is kept only for image-to-video; Gemini CLI is a draft long-context lane. Third-party per-image APIs (Replicate etc.) and web-UI-only tools (Kling, Magnific/Seedream) were removed from routing on 2026-07-13 — their benchmark findings live on in benchmarks/ and references/, and web-UI quality winners may return as human_options if the need recurs.

claude-sub.reset_windows: 5h rolling + 7d weekly + per-model weekly scoped caps

auth_probe.method: ~/.claude/.credentials.json .claudeAiOauth.expiresAt (epoch ms) > now; plan from .claudeAiOauth.subscriptionType/.rateLimitTier; macOS fallback: Keychain item 'Claude Code-credentials'. (Manual extra: ~/.claude.json .oauthAccount carries org/billing fields the doctor does not read)
auth_probe.source: local file inspection + claude v2.1.207 binary strings, 2026-07-13

usage_probe.method: GET https://api.anthropic.com/api/oauth/usage with 'Authorization: Bearer <accessToken>' + 'anthropic-beta: oauth-2025-04-20' -> five_hour/seven_day .utilization + .resets_at, limits[] (kind weekly_scoped = per-model caps), extra_usage
usage_probe.scope: account-wide (server-metered across all machines and surfaces)
usage_probe.caveats: upstream stuck-429 bug (anthropics/claude-code#30930, #31021, #31637): cache success 180s, back off 30min on error, never clobber last-good data
usage_probe.source: verified live 2026-07-13; endpoint string present in claude v2.1.207 binary; same data as the in-app /usage screen

codex-sub.reset_windows: primary ~5h + secondary weekly (both delivered as server-header snapshots)

auth_probe.method: `codex login status` exit 0/1 (mode string on stderr); plan tier: base64url-decode $CODEX_HOME/auth.json tokens.id_token middle segment -> claim 'https://api.openai.com/auth'.chatgpt_plan_type (undocumented-but-stable; unknown-plan on parse failure). (Manual fallback the doctor does not run: `codex doctor --json` checks['auth.credentials'])
auth_probe.source: openai/codex HEAD bb947e8e + live CLI behavior, 2026-07-13

usage_probe.method: reverse-scan newest $CODEX_HOME/sessions/YYYY/MM/DD/rollout-*.jsonl for the last {type:'event_msg', payload.type:'token_count'} line with non-null payload.rate_limits -> primary/secondary {used_percent, window_minutes, resets_at (unix s)}, credits, plan_type; live alternative (--deep): `codex app-server` JSON-RPC account/rateLimits/read
usage_probe.scope: rate_limits mirror x-codex-* server headers (account-wide numbers), but the snapshot is only as fresh as the last codex turn on THIS machine — always surface its timestamp
usage_probe.source: openai/codex codex-rs rollout/src/policy.rs, protocol.rs TokenCountEvent, codex-api/src/rate_limits.rs, HEAD bb947e8e 2026-07-13

gemini.kind: per-auth-path (API-key metered/free-capped, Vertex, org Code Assist seats)
gemini.reset_windows: daily request caps per auth path (API-key free tier 250 req/day Flash-only; Code Assist Standard 1,500/day, Enterprise 2,000/day — vendor docs, checked 2026-07-13)

auth_probe.method: ~/.gemini/settings.json .security.auth.selectedType (oauth-personal|gemini-api-key|vertex-ai), corroborated by oauth_creds.json refresh_token, google_accounts.json .active, or GEMINI_API_KEY env. POLICY KNOWLEDGE: oauth-personal without an org Code Assist seat (GOOGLE_CLOUD_PROJECT) is server-blocked since 2026-06-18 (ALL individual tiers — free, AI Pro, AI Ultra — any CLI version) -> verdict blocked; --deep liveness probe re-verifies (exit 41 = FatalAuthenticationError)
auth_probe.source: gemini-cli source (storage.ts, oauth2.ts, settingsSchema.ts, errors.ts) + Google deprecation notice (updated 2026-06-23), checked 2026-07-13

usage_probe.method: no cheap standing readout on the routable (API-key) path: per-run token stats via --output-format json (stats.models.<id>.tokens); org Code Assist seats can POST cloudcode-pa.googleapis.com/v1internal:retrieveUserQuota; quota errors are typed (TerminalQuotaError = daily cap, RetryableQuotaError = per-minute). NEVER send /stats headlessly — it reaches the model as a prompt, not a command
usage_probe.scope: per-run only on the API-key path
usage_probe.source: gemini-cli json-formatter.ts, uiTelemetry.ts, googleQuotaErrors.ts, code_assist/server.ts, checked 2026-07-13

grok.kind: subscription (SuperGrok: ONE shared metered weekly pool across Chat/Imagine/Voice/Build since June 2026 — no longer flat/unmetered)
grok.reset_windows: weekly shared pool; percent + reset date at grok.com Settings -> Usage (human surface)

auth_probe.method: $GROK_HOME/auth.json (default ~/.grok/) has an entry with non-expired expires_at (top-level keys are OIDC scope URLs; ~7-day tokens, CLI auto-refreshes); XAI_API_KEY env is the alternative auth path — NOTE it is metered per-call (api.x.ai billing), NOT the weekly subscription pool. Do NOT trust `grok models` exit code for auth — it exits 0 signed-out; a real headless probe prints {"type":"error","message":"Not signed in..."} on stdout and exits 1

usage_probe.method: no stable scriptable pool-percent readout (the TUI /usage command just opens grok.com/?_s=usage in a browser — no headless subcommand exists; the ACP x.ai/billing JSON-RPC method returns -32601 Method-not-found on 0.2.99, live-verified 2026-07-13; the gRPC-web billing endpoint some trackers use needs browser cookies and is unofficial/drift-prone). Headless JSON stamps per-run usage/modelUsage/total_cost_usd fields since 0.2.97 — but cost fields are often OMITTED on subscription/OAuth runs (cost_is_partial): absence means unreported, never free. Free/X-Basic tier gate: media tools hard-fail with 'Do not retry this tool' — treat as lane-unavailable, never retry
usage_probe.scope: per-run only; pool-level percent is human-web-only
usage_probe.source: grok 0.2.99 live runs + grok.com shared-usage FAQ, 2026-07-13

anthropic/fable-5.when: architecture, briefs, final review, synthesis, judgment. Effort is the throttle: thinking is always on, so hard tasks run hotter than the weight suggests.

anthropic/opus-4.8.when: diverse second-perspective reviews, judge/verify stages. A different model reviewing the author's work beats same-model review (correlated blind spots).

api_per_mtok.source: claude.com/pricing, checked 2026-07-11 — intro pricing until 2026-08-31, then 3/15

anthropic/sonnet-5.when: workhorse subagents (Agent/Workflow default), drafting, bounded recon.

anthropic/haiku-4.5.efforts_source: manual thinking budgets, not the effort parameter (platform.claude.com adaptive-thinking docs)
anthropic/haiku-4.5.when: mechanical contract-gated pulls ONLY — never judgment. The contract (fabrication-proof brief, verifiable outputs) is what makes this lane safe.

openai/gpt-5.5.quota_weight_note: 0 is a state, not a property: it holds while codex reset headroom far exceeds draw. Tripwire: when codex tokens/resets run low, re-weight (~2-3) and re-balance.
openai/gpt-5.5.efforts_source: estimated — set via ~/.codex/config.toml model_reasoning_effort or -c flag; full accepted set unverified from primary sources as of 2026-07-11
openai/gpt-5.5.when: typing, recon, web research, image generation, HTML/codegen. Pass -m gpt-5.5 explicitly: the CLI default (gpt-5.6-sol) lost a 2026-07-11 side-by-side audition on real briefs.

openai/gpt-image-2.when: raster image generation via codex exec (selected by the phrase 'GPT Image 2.0' in the prompt — no model flag exists).

google/gemini-cli.when: bulk reading and native audio/video/PDF ingestion via the Gemini CLI — API-key (GEMINI_API_KEY), Vertex, or org Code Assist seat auth ONLY: all individual Google-account tiers (free, AI Pro, AI Ultra) were blocked server-side on 2026-06-18, on every CLI version. Model tier varies by auth path; unbenchmarked.
google/gemini-cli.cost_note: quota_weight null = draws a separate pool per auth path (API-key free tier 250 req/day Flash-only; Code Assist Standard 1,500/day, Enterprise 2,000/day — vendor docs 2026-07-13), not the Claude/Codex pools; see pools.gemini and references/setup.md

xai/grok-media.when: image_to_video previz on a SuperGrok subscription. Since June 2026 every call draws the ONE shared metered weekly pool (Chat/Imagine/Voice/Build) — no longer flat/unmetered as originally recorded. (Its image_edit tool benchmarked well — 4.7, grok-kling 2026-07-10 — but the lane is out of scope for now.)
xai/grok-media.cost_note: quota_weight null = separate weekly shared pool with no scriptable percent readout (grok.com Settings -> Usage is the human surface); see pools.grok

image-generation.description: Text-to-image: illustrations, POD/print designs, mockups, text-in-scene graphics.

score.notes: Won 19/20 archetypes vs Flux 2 Pro (4.38), Ideogram v3 (3.98), Nano-Banana 2 (3.80). 0 misspellings/60 incl. Japanese. ~10x slower (~119s/img).

gotchas: prompts over ~150 words TIME OUT, not just slow down
aspect-ratio drift to poster orientation — lead prompt with 'Format:'
naming a living artist returns 'stream disconnected' (moderation disguised as rate limit)
no transparency ever — opaque white-BG RGB, plan a background-removal hop
~1.5MP native; upscale (4x Real-ESRGAN) for large prints
no character memory — anchor every frame with a reference PNG

image-to-video.description: Animate a start frame into a short clip.

invocation.command_template: grok --no-auto-update --always-approve --output-format json --single "<motion prompt; reference the start frame by ABSOLUTE path — the CLI's image_to_video tool takes image=<abs path>, duration 6|10s>"

artifact.format: mp4 (6|10s, 448-672px); lands session-relative at videos/N.mp4 — the tool result reports the absolute path, capture it from the JSON output

cost.est_per_call: draws the shared weekly SuperGrok pool (metered since June 2026); on XAI_API_KEY auth it is per-call metered instead — the doctor reports auth.method

score.notes: Motion-direction-following competitive with Kling (push-in/turn/orbit/walk all read correctly). The previz / high-volume low-stakes lane.

gotchas: 448-672px output — previz only, not client-facing
moderation non-deterministic, ~1 in 6-8 calls blocked (domestic framing + flash/minimal-garment combos)
grok image_gen takes NO reference input — never substitute it when the start frame carries identity
always pass --no-auto-update in scripted runs (update chatter goes to stderr; JSON stays on stdout)
free/X-Basic tier: media tools hard-fail with 'Do not retry this tool' — lane-unavailable, not a retryable error (2026-07-13)
each call draws the shared weekly SuperGrok pool — batch previz waves with the weekly reset date in mind (grok.com Settings -> Usage)

code-recon.description: Read-only codebase investigation: diagnosis, root-cause chains, architecture mapping.

invocation.command_template: codex exec -m gpt-5.5 -C <dir> -s read-only --ephemeral -o out.txt - < brief.md

cost.est_per_call: ~10k tokens minimum per exec — batch questions

score.notes: Recon at xhigh effort is excellent (correct root-cause chains with accurate file:line refs; detects already-fixed bugs).

gotchas: literal constants are the weak spot — verify every number at file:line
NEGATIVE claims ('X does not exist') get ZERO trust until the caller probes obvious candidates
per-exec overhead ~10k tokens — never use for one-liners, batch into one session

invocation.command_template: Agent tool (model: sonnet) inside Claude Code; from other CLIs: claude -p --model sonnet --effort medium "<self-contained brief>" > out.md

score.notes: UNBENCHMARKED as a routed lane. Use when the codex pool is exhausted or the recon must stay inside the Claude toolchain.

gotchas: same trust calibration as any delegated run: constants and negative claims are unverified until checked
spawning a fresh subagent per question re-pays the context write — batch questions, reuse persistent workers (cache reads ~0.1x)

web-research.description: Cited web research: multi-source reports, tool/library due diligence, fact-finding. Split from code-recon because the failure modes differ: citation accuracy and negative-claim probing, not literal constants.

invocation.command_template: codex exec -m gpt-5.5 -s read-only --ephemeral --skip-git-repo-check -c tools.web_search=true -o out.txt - < brief.md

score.notes: No overall computed yet (candidate for web-research-v1). Field record: ~2k-word cited reports in ~10 min; ~1 factual error per 40 citations (a license misreported); honest UNVERIFIED flags; refused to fabricate a nonexistent system.

gotchas: NEGATIVE claims require caller probing: it once reported a 24.9k-star repo 'does not exist' because the brief hyphenated the name — seed briefs with name permutations
verify cited URLs cheaply without LLM tokens: curl -sIL liveness + GitHub API for stars/license
literal constants (versions, prices, license names) are the weak spot — spot-check against primary sources

invocation.command_template: Agent tool (model: sonnet, WebSearch/WebFetch enabled) with a research brief demanding citations and UNVERIFIED flags

artifact.format: markdown report — caller captures agent's final message

gotchas: demand per-claim citations and UNVERIFIED markers in the brief — the report format is only as honest as the brief requires

code-implementation.description: Delegated implementation: typing out well-briefed changes in an isolated worktree.

artifact.format: git worktree + branch; caller reviews diff and merges

score.notes: 6/6 packages first-shot green with strong tests in the best wave. Review is NEVER delegated: read the full diff, run gates yourself.

gotchas: worktrees branch from ORIGIN/main — local-only commits are invisible to it; push or copy files first
sandbox can leave .git read-only — Codex does file work, caller does ALL git index/commit work
changed exported signatures: audit ALL call sites, it silently truncated two once
inline exact literal values and a SAMPLE of any data file into the brief — it assumed a flat shape for a nested JSON it never sampled

review-second-opinion.description: Independent review of work by a DIFFERENT model than the author: design critiques, PR second passes, judge/verify stages. Same-model review has correlated blind spots — pick the provider from a different model family than the author (Claude-authored work -> the codex reviewer; Codex-authored work -> the opus reviewer).

invocation.command_template: Agent tool (model: opus) with the artifact under review + explicit deliverables; from other CLIs: claude -p --model opus "<review brief>" > review.md

artifact.format: markdown review — caller captures agent's final message

score.notes: UNBENCHMARKED as a routed lane. Field record 2026-07-11: independent Opus critique of this pack's v2 design surfaced the probes-lie caveat and the sparse-scores discipline that the authoring model's own pass missed. 2026-07-14: routed Opus review of the doctor script found a real misroute bug (in-session lapsed-token false-negative) that a 23-agent same-model review pass had missed — a different MODEL adds signal even within the same family.

gotchas: give the reviewer the raw artifact, not the author's summary of it — summaries launder the author's blind spots into the review

invocation.command_template: codex exec -m gpt-5.5 -C <dir> -s read-only --ephemeral -o review.md - < review-brief.md

score.notes: UNBENCHMARKED as a review lane (same invocation shape as the benchmarked code-recon lane). Exists so Claude-AUTHORED work can get a genuinely different-model review — routing Claude's work to claude/subagent-opus reproduces the correlated-blind-spots problem this capability exists to avoid.

gotchas: same trust calibration as codex recon: verify literal constants at file:line; negative claims ('no bug here') get zero trust without caller probing
give it the raw diff/artifact in the brief, never the author's summary — and demand file:line evidence per finding

long-context-multimodal.description: Tasks needing very large context windows or native audio/video/PDF input.

invocation.command_template: gemini -p "<prompt>" --output-format json (see references/longcontext.md for file-attachment patterns)

score.notes: UNBENCHMARKED — drafted lane. First candidate for a pack-run suite (longcontext-v1).

gotchas: reference is a draft: verify invocation flags against the installed gemini CLI version before relying on it

ALL individual Google-account tiers (free, AI Pro, AI Ultra) are blocked server-side since 2026-06-18, on every CLI version (IneligibleTierError points at the Antigravity successor); the lane routes only with GEMINI_API_KEY, Vertex, or an org Code Assist seat (+GOOGLE_CLOUD_PROJECT)
use --output-format json headlessly: exit 41 = auth failure; stats.models.<id>.tokens is the per-run usage meter; NEVER send /stats as a prompt — it reaches the model as text

invocation.command_template: Agent tool (model: sonnet) with the bulk material chunked into self-contained read-and-distill briefs; from other CLIs: claude -p --model sonnet --effort low "<brief>" < chunk.md > out.md

cost.est_per_call: quota_weight 3 per token; fan-out reading multiplies draw

score.notes: UNBENCHMARKED but FIELD-RUN (2026-07-14: two-chunk research-corpus distillation dogfood; both workers held the contract — confidence tags preserved, and the open-questions demand surfaced a real contradiction between sources that a single-pass read had missed). Text-only and a smaller window than Gemini — chunk the material and distill per chunk. Field anchor: orchestrated bulk reading reached ~96% of solo quality at ~46% of cost (BrowseComp; see SKILL.md step 2).

gotchas: no native audio/video/PDF ingestion — extract text first, or report that media understanding has no routable lane

chunk to fit the subagent window; demand per-chunk citations plus an explicit open-questions list, or cross-chunk links silently drop (2026-07-14: the open-questions list is what caught a real cross-source contradiction)
chunk overlap produces near-duplicate facts across distillates — the caller dedupes when stitching (observed 2026-07-14)
capture the worker's final message to a file WHEN IT RETURNS — recovering it later means parsing transcript JSONL for the last assistant text block (observed 2026-07-14)
